title: Uterine contraction device
abstract: The present disclosure describes a uterine contraction device which may be utilized to treat and/or prevent postpartum hemorrhaging. The uterine contraction device includes a catheter and a pressure seal adjustably coupled to the catheter. A distal section of the catheter is configured for insertion into the uterus, and a proximal end of the catheter is configured for attachment to a vacuum device. Negative pressure generated by the vacuum device is transmitted to the distal section, while the pressure seal forms a seal at the vaginal introitus to maintain the negative pressure within the uterus to assist in contracting the uterus.
claims: Claims 1. A medical device configured for insertion into the uterus to aid in contracting the uterus, the device comprising: a catheter having a proximal end, a distal end, and a wall defining a lumen, the catheter being configured such that when inserted within a patient, the proximal end remains outside of the patient, and at least a portion of a distal section extends into the uterus, wherein at least a portion of the distal section includes a plurality of perforations extending through the catheter wall to expose the lumen of the catheter, and wherein the proximal end is attachable to a vacuum device to enable transmission of negative pressure through the lumen to the distal section; and a pressure seal coupled to the catheter such that when the catheter is inserted into the patient, the pressure seal is disposed within the vaginal canal, the pressure seal having a bell shape that opens towards the proximal end of the catheter. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the pressure seal is adjustably coupled to the catheter such that the pressure seal is translatable along a longitudinal axis of the catheter. 3. The device of claim 2, further comprising a locking mechanism to lock a selected position of the pressure seal relative to the catheter. 4. The device of claim 3, wherein the locking mechanism includes one or more grooves disposed on the catheter and one or more corresponding extensions disposed within a conduit of the pressure seal, the one or more extensions being configured to engage with the one or more grooves to lock relative position of the pressure seal upon the catheter. 5. The device of claim 3, wherein the locking mechanism includes a keyway disposed on the catheter and one or more corresponding keys disposed within a conduit of the pressure seal, the keyway including a plurality of offset slots for receiving the one or more keys upon rotation of the pressure seal to move the keys from the keyway into the offset slots. 6. The device of claim 3, wherein the locking mechanism includes friction fitting of the pressure seal and the catheter. 7. The device of claim 1, wherein the pressure seal includes a plurality of ridges disposed upon an outer surface of the pressure seal. 8. The device of claim 6, wherein the ridges are circumferentially oriented about the outer surface of the pressure seal. 9. The device of claim 1, wherein the catheter includes a proximal section formed separately from the distal section, the proximal section and the distal section being attached to form the catheter. 10. The device of claim 9, wherein the proximal section is formed from a material having a higher modulus of elasticity than the distal section. 11. The device of claim 10, wherein the pressure seal is formed from a material having a modulus of elasticity intermediate between that of the distal section and that of the proximal section. 12. A medical device configured for insertion into the uterus to aid in contracting the uterus, the device comprising: a catheter having a proximal end, a distal end, and a wall defining a lumen, the catheter being configured such that when inserted within a patient, the proximal end remains outside of the patient, and at least a portion of a distal section extends into the uterus, wherein at least a portion of the distal section includes a plurality of perforations extending through the catheter wall to expose the lumen of the catheter, and wherein the proximal end is attachable to a vacuum device to enable transmission of negative pressure through the lumen to the distal section; and a pressure seal adjustably coupled to the proximal section of the catheter such that the pressure seal is translatable along a longitudinal axis of the catheter. 13. The device of claim 12, further comprising a locking mechanism to lock a selected position of the pressure seal relative to the catheter. 14. The device of claim 13, wherein the locking mechanism includes one or more grooves disposed on the catheter and one or more corresponding extensions disposed within a conduit of the pressure seal, the one or more extensions being configured to engage with the one or more grooves to lock relative position of the pressure seal upon the catheter. 15. The device of claim 13, wherein the locking mechanism includes friction fitting of the pressure seal and the catheter. 16. The device of claim 12, wherein the pressure seal includes a plurality of ridges circumferentially oriented about an outer surface of the pressure seal. 17. The device of claim 12, wherein the catheter includes a proximal section formed separately from the distal section, the proximal section and the distal section being attached to form the catheter. 18. A method of treating postpartum hemorrhaging in a patient, the method comprising: providing a medical device, the medical device including: a catheter having a proximal end, a distal end, a distal section adjacent to the distal end, and a wall defining a lumen, the catheter being configured such that when inserted within a patient, the proximal end remains outside of the patient, and the distal section extends into the uterus, wherein at least a portion of the distal section includes a plurality of perforations extending through the catheter wall to expose the lumen of the catheter, and wherein the proximal end is attachable to a vacuum device to enable transmission of negative pressure through the lumen to the distal section; and a pressure seal adjustably coupled to the proximal section of the catheter such that the pressure seal is translatable along a longitudinal axis of the catheter; inserting the distal section of the medical device into the patient's uterus; positioning the pressure seal at the patient's vaginal introitus; and applying vacuum pressure to aid in contracting the uterus. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the pressure seal has a bell shape that opens towards the proximal end of the catheter. 20. The method of claim 18, wherein the medical device further includes a locking mechanism to lock a selected position of the pressure seal relative to the catheter, the method further comprising locking the pressure seal in position prior to applying vacuum pressure.
